verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is a canonical secreted serine protease with a signal peptide; no TM domain, no GPI anchor, no stable membrane-anchoring complex partner. No evidence of cell-state-induced surface display, tissue-restricted PM localization, lysosomal exocytosis cycling, or PM residence. Therapeutic targeting (anti-KLK2 antibodies / bispecifics) engages the soluble circulating form in serum, not a surface-anchored pool. KLK2 is secreted-only, not surface-accessible at the protein body.
